title: Google’s new AI agent stack from I/O 2026
summary: Google announced at I/O 2026 a four-layer AI agent stack featuring Gemini 3.5 Flash, Agent Development Kit 2.0 with graph-based orchestration, the Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol for interoperability, and Managed Agents API for production deployment. The stack includes Anti-Gravity 2.0, enterprise skill registries, and Gemini Omni Flash for multimodal video understanding.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech video walks through the AI agent stack Google unveiled at I/O 2026, covering the components and how they fit together. The centerpiece is the Gemini 3.5 Flash model, paired with Agent Development Kit 2.0 and its graph-based orchestration, the Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol for standardized communication between agents, and the Managed Agents API. The stack also adds developer tooling: an agent CLI, enterprise skill registries, and the Gemini Omni Flash multimodal video model. The takeaway is a consolidated set of models, protocols, and tools aimed at building and coordinating multi-agent systems.
